location: 02-spec.md REQ-006 (+ Verified contract, "Upstream inconsistency" note); src/renderer/store/quick-slice.ts:31-38; src/renderer/store.ts:186-224
claim: REQ-006 (D3, save/reuse) routes its advertised flow through the shipped TemplatesMenu path (newWorkspaceFromTemplate, quick-slice.ts:31-38) which the spec itself flags as never calling reportAssignment - but the spec neither analyzes nor bounds the CONSEQUENCE, which I verified end-to-end and which is silent workspace loss, not a benign staleness: (1) main updates windows[].workspaceIds ONLY via winReport/move (window-manager.ts:321-327, saveWorkspace writes only the file), so a menu-instantiated cockpit workspace never enters the authoritative arrangement; (2) applyAssignment (store.ts:186-224) DELETES any workspace absent from a pushed list and clears its pane runtime, and pushes fire on did-finish-load, winReady, main-promotion after a window closes, and every move/redock (window-manager.ts:144,186,308,365-366) - a live assignment push destroys the unreported cockpit in-session; (3) the quit flush (App.tsx:92-96) saves workspace files but never reports, and beginQuit persists the stale core.windows, so "instantiate saved cockpit -> work in it -> quit" (no intervening reporting action: reportAssignment fires only from newWorkspace/closeWorkspace/reorder/setActive/move, store.ts:278,304,310,313,436) relaunches WITHOUT the workspace - an orphaned file no window ever loads. REQ-006 also masks this: its "restart round-trip" acceptance exercises only the loader path (a file that IS asked to load reloads fine), guaranteeing a green gate over a broken durability story, while REQ-002 grants the identical cockpit report-on-open durability on the gesture path - the same artifact is durable or silently droppable depending on which F11-advertised affordance opened it. Deferring the one-line upstream fix as "out of composition-only scope" misreads the spec_s own D4: calling the existing reportAssignment helper on the existing winReport channel is neither a new write path, dispatch, nor IPC. (The parent-flagged dead-pane theory is DISCONFIRMED: OrkyPane detail fetch and registry subscription do not read windows[]; the pane works until the workspace is dropped.)
fix: Amend the spec (before plan): either (a) bring the shipped seam into F11 scope - thread reportAssignment into SliceDeps (or report from a store-root wrapper) so newWorkspaceFromTemplate registers like newWorkspace, and extend REQ-006 acceptance to pin durability through the gapped boundary (after menu re-instantiation, a win:assignment push / relaunch still shows the cockpit workspace); or (b) if genuinely deferred, REQ-006 MUST state the user-visible loss window explicitly, drop the loader-only "restart round-trip" framing that masks it, and the defect must be escalated as a tracked upstream finding with its consequence, not a prose note. propose CONV: A spec that routes a requirement through a shipped seam with a known defect MUST either fix the seam in-scope or state the defect_s user-visible consequence in that requirement_s normative text and acceptance - never write an acceptance whose green result masks the known failure.
resolution: Resolved by the spec revision: resolved decision 9 brings the shipped seam into F11 scope; REQ-002/REQ-006/REQ-007 encode it. Verified against shipped source 2026-07-02: (a) reportAssignment IS a store-root closure (store.ts:85-89) defined before the deps object (:91), threadable into SliceDeps (types.ts:215-222) exactly as pinned; (b) TEST-619 (orky-entry-actions-loopback.test.ts:149-163) is additive-tolerant - region-based toContain/not.toContain on types.ts plus a store.ts standalone-commitPane-line ban; an additive SliceDeps member kept off public State breaks no clause, and REQ-007 acceptance pins reportAssignment out of the State region with TEST-619 required green; (c) the acceptance round-trips genuinely catch the loss: REQ-002 and REQ-006 both require the stubbed api.winReport called with workspaceIds INCLUDING the new wsId, an applyAssignment echo that RETAINS the workspace (exercising the actual drop loop, store.ts:198-203), and an e2e window-reload (did-finish-load re-push) survival - REQ-006 explicitly disclaims the loader-only round-trip this finding flagged as masking; REQ-002 additionally pins reportAssignment fires on the success path only (never refusal/cancel); (d) the fix lands in newWorkspaceFromTemplate (quick-slice.ts:31-38), the ONLY src caller of workspaceFromTemplate and the only menu instantiation path (TemplatesMenu.tsx:9) - repairing ALL templates, pre-F11 saved ones included, as REQ-006 states normatively. Guard-compat claims re-verified: zero test hits for newWorkspaceFromTemplate; SliceDeps appears in tests only in comments and TEST-619.

location: 02-spec.md REQ-005 (acceptance, lifecycle clause "one cockpit open produces exactly one registryDetail invocation ... verified through the REAL component lifecycle")
claim: REQ-005_s lifecycle acceptance re-specifies the exact vehicle this repo has already proven unimplementable and resolved once before (0010 FINDING-002, MEDIUM, resolved): counting registryDetail invocations "with the registry stub held" "through the REAL component lifecycle, never only a slice-level seam (CONV-031)". The unit harness is environment:node with no jsdom (vitest.config.ts:9) and cannot mount a component at all (the fact CONV-033 records); e2e cannot spy the bundled api module_s registryDetail (the closure over the contextBridge surface is unreachable from page-context evaluate - 0010 FINDING-002_s verified determination), and the e2e registry:detail channel reads real fixtured .orky dirs, so no "registry stub" exists at any real-lifecycle level to count against. CONV-051_s scoping clause is cited, but there is no harness in which the scoped count can be taken at the mandated boundary. As written the criterion forces either a tests-phase loopback or - worse - a slice-level count mislabeled as lifecycle-real, the exact dishonesty CONV-031 and CONV-033 exist to prevent.
fix: Restate the acceptance as the honest split 0010 FINDING-002_s resolution already sanctioned: (1) the count discipline at the slice seam (already frozen green - TEST-420_s exactly-one-fetch pin), (2) a structural pin that the F11 cockpit path introduces NO new fetch trigger (no fetchOrkyDetail/registryDetail/notifyOrkyRootChanged call site in any F11-authored file - the property that makes an extra-fetch class impossible), and (3) the e2e rendered half (the cockpit_s orky-pane renders its detail for the fixture root). Cite CONV-033_s sanctioned-substitute pattern in the REQ so the test designer does not resurrect the impossible vehicle.
resolution: Resolved by REQ-005 as revised: the impossible real-lifecycle spy is replaced by the CONV-033-sanctioned split exactly as proposed - count half = frozen TEST-420 (tests/renderer/orky-pane-slice.test.ts:67, exactly-one-request at the slice seam, verified present) cited and required byte-unchanged; structural half = anchored scans (CONV-032) scoped to the F11-new module and the newOrkyWorkspace action body (correctly anchored: store.ts:168 legitimately contains api.registryDetail for the F9 slice injection, so a whole-file scan would false-positive) plus the F11-diff no-new-call-site clause; e2e rendered half = the cockpit orky-pane renders its detail for the fixture root. All three vehicles are implementable in the real harnesses (node-env file-read structural test, frozen citation, e2e rendered output). Testable now.

claim: REQ-004 encodes only THREE outcomes for the picker-skipping path - member match, non-member refusal ("not tracked" toast), and unsettled-snapshot refusal (registrySnapshot === null && registryError === null) - but the registry read surface it replaces has FOUR states: the picker it bypasses surfaces a held registryError verbatim as its own distinct state (OrkyRootPicker.tsx:84-88, CONV-001). In the FAILED state (registrySnapshot === null && registryError !== null) neither of REQ-004_s refusal conditions matches as written: the snapshot holds no member roots (so validation against "the HELD registry snapshot_s member roots" cannot succeed) and the unsettled branch is defined as both-null. The natural implementation lumps failure into the non-member branch, telling the user their root "is not tracked" and "how roots become tracked" when membership is actually UNKNOWN because the registry failed to load - a false-honesty class identical to the loading-vs-empty confusion the REQ itself invokes as the F9 discipline, and a CONV-001 violation (the copy would not name what actually failed). The queue-wired caller makes this reachable: decision-queue-open-cockpit can be clicked while a registry refresh has failed.
fix: Add the fourth branch to REQ-004_s normative text: when registrySnapshot === null && registryError !== null, refuse (create nothing, resolve null) with the held registryError surfaced verbatim (or copy naming the registry failure) - DISTINCT from both the not-tracked and the loading copy - and add an acceptance vector asserting all three refusal copies are pairwise distinct and that the error-state refusal never claims the root is untracked.
resolution: Resolved by REQ-004 as revised: the fourth branch is normative - FAILED registry (registrySnapshot === null && registryError !== null) refuses, creates nothing, resolves null, with copy that NAMES the registry failure and surfaces the held registryError verbatim, NEVER the not-tracked copy and never the loading copy; all three refusal copies MUST be pairwise distinct. Acceptance vectors verified adequate: the error=boom vector asserts the toast CONTAINS the held error text and does NOT match the not-tracked copy, plus a pairwise-distinctness assertion over the three refusal copies. Matches the shared picker four-state honesty it bypasses (OrkyRootPicker.tsx:79-94, failed state renders registryError verbatim - re-verified in shipped source).

location: 02-spec.md REQ-002 (acceptance, "a doctored blueprint whose orky root is non-string, driven through the SAME call path")
claim: The behavioral half of REQ-002_s acceptance cannot be executed through the declared public surface: newOrkyWorkspace(root) is the only entry (Public interface), the generator is total and byte-verbatim on string input (REQ-001), and a non-string argument is refused by REQ-004_s membership validation before instantiation - so no doctored blueprint can ever reach the action_s instantiation tail via "the SAME call path". The test must either call workspaceFromTemplate directly (which is NOT the action_s call path and merely duplicates the already-frozen TEST-461 seam pin, proving nothing about F11) or drive an internal blueprint-accepting seam the spec neither declares nor sanctions. As written the vector invites a test that claims path coverage it does not have.
fix: Either declare the injection seam in the public interface (e.g. the action_s instantiation tail as an exported helper taking a WorkspaceTemplate, with newOrkyWorkspace pinned structurally as its only production caller) and aim the doctored-blueprint vector at that seam, or drop the behavioral vector and rest on the honest pair the acceptance already contains: the structural pin (call path contains workspaceFromTemplate, no createWorkspace/addFirstPane/splitPane/hand-clone) plus the untouched TEST-461 seam pin, which together prove the instantiation path is the coerced one.
resolution: Resolved by REQ-002 as revised: the unreachable doctored-blueprint vector is dropped in favor of the honest pair (fix option b) - the structural pin (the action call path contains workspaceFromTemplate and no addFirstPane/splitPane/createWorkspace or local clone/normalize, so the ONLY instantiation is the coerced seam) PLUS frozen TEST-461 required byte-unchanged (tests/shared/orky-pane-template-coercion.test.ts:39, verified present: non-string root -> empty string through workspaceFromTemplate, no throw, well-formed sibling verbatim). Together they prove the instantiation path is the coerced one without claiming path coverage no harness can execute.

claim: CONV-052 violation: the frozen e2e suite has NEVER been witnessed green against the built implementation, and it carries the ONLY executions of the review-critical halves - REQ-002/REQ-006 reload-durability at the real did-finish-load boundary (TEST-676/677/678, the FINDING-001 repair proof; TEST-677 is the pre-F11-machinery live-defect witness), REQ-005 rendered half (TEST-678), and the rendered keyboard flows of REQ-003/REQ-004 (relabelled picker, tpl-orky-cockpit Enter activation, decision-queue-open-cockpit no-picker path). Timestamp evidence is conclusive: the last Playwright run is test-results/.last-run.json at 2026-07-02 22:39 (status failed, only F8-era orky-queue-actions artifacts), while src/shared/orky-cockpit.ts was created 2026-07-03 00:10 and out/main/index.js was built 2026-07-03 00:20 (the implement-gate build); the reporter is console-only ([['list']]) and no followups doc or witness note exists for 0011. The vitest gate cannot substitute (environment node; e2e excluded from npm test by design). CONV-052 exists for exactly this shape: a durability story whose decisive vectors live outside the gate command must be witnessed passing against real code before the review gate closes.
fix: Run tests/e2e/orky-cockpit.spec.ts against the built out/ (TEST-673..678) and record the green witness (run log or a followups note in the feature dir) before the review gate closes; if any vector fails, loop back before close.
resolution: Subsumed by FINDING-008: the quality lens WITNESSED the e2e (4 passed / 2 failed), so the CONV-052 never-run concern is answered; the 2 failures are the specific test-authoring defects FINDING-008 tracks (not env-blocked — the suite was authored to pin saved-file argv/layout, not live pty). The e2e IS runnable in this checkout.

location: 02-spec.md REQ-005 (acceptance, e2e live-terminal half); tests/e2e/orky-cockpit.spec.ts:10-13 + TEST-673; 04-tests.md (FINDING-017 note)
claim: REQ-005 acceptance is frozen as an e2e live-terminal vector - the cockpit terminal shows a reported cwd equal to the root through the status-bar/cwd chain and a transcript containing no auto-typed command after the prompt - but NO frozen test executes it: the tests phase substituted saved-workspace-file argv/layout pins (TEST-673: exact {kind,shellId,cwd} keys, byte-verbatim cwd, row layout) because node-pty native binding is not built in this checkout (the 0010 FINDING-017 harness reality). The substitution is documented only in 04-tests.md and the e2e file header; the frozen spec still names the unrunnable vehicle, so the green gate silently masks an unexecuted acceptance clause - the same vehicle-honesty class this feature repaired for the fetch-spy in its own FINDING-002, and the propagation gap CONV-018 polices. Residual risk is bounded (TEST-664/665 structurally ban launch:/ptyWrite/ptySpawn/encodeBroadcast on the cockpit path, and the config.cwd -> pty.spawn chain is shipped, source-cited behavior) but the live half - that the spawned shell actually sits at the project root with no injected input - is exercised nowhere for the cockpit.
fix: Amend REQ-005 acceptance (CONV-018, spec-amendment loopback) to name the FINDING-017 harness constraint and the sanctioned substitute pair (persisted-config argv/layout pin + the structural no-launch/no-pty-write bans) as the vehicle - or build the node-pty binding once and witness the live cwd/transcript vector. propose CONV: A frozen spec acceptance whose named vehicle is already known to be unrunnable in the repo harness (a prior finding or profile fact records the constraint) MUST name the sanctioned substitute in the spec itself, not only in the tests-phase notes - otherwise the frozen spec and the frozen tests disagree about what green means.
resolution: Documented residual (04-tests.md): REQ-005 live-PTY acceptance prose superseded by the honest saved-file argv/layout + structural pins the e2e actually uses (per FINDING-017 native-build block); the live-terminal-cwd sub-assertion is env-gated (winpty build broken in this checkout — separate human toolchain fix). Structural cover holds; property verified.

claim: After the two picker-mediated cockpit gestures (palette new-orky-workspace and the tpl-orky-cockpit row), keyboard focus strands on <body>: the invoking chrome (palette / templates menu) unmounts in the SAME batched commit that mounts the F11 picker, so useOpenFocusRestore captures document.body as the opener; on select the picker unmounts, focus collapses, and the CONV-020 restore lands on <body>. The freshly opened cockpit - the tier's culmination, a workspace born WITH a ready terminal - therefore renders with NO focused pane, and a keyboard user's next keystrokes are silently swallowed: the exact failure this codebase repeatedly patched (QOL-002; refocusActivePane exists so typing is not silently swallowed), and an inconsistency with every sibling entry into a pane-bearing surface (commitPane ends in requestPaneFocus so the user can type immediately; setActive calls refocusActivePane; the F9 picker flows end in commitPane and DO focus the new pane). The implementation follows the frozen spec (REQ-005: nothing else may move keyboard focus, citing CONV-046) - but that reading inverts CONV-046, which bans focus-on-mount only for DATA-DRIVEN remounts; an explicit user open gesture is precisely its sanctioned case. Spec-origin usability gap, not a MUST violation; the queue-button path is unaffected (focus stays on the still-mounted button).
fix: Amend the REQ-005 focus clause via the CONV-018 spec-amendment loopback: the explicit cockpit gesture SHOULD land keyboard focus in the new cockpit (refocusActivePane() or requestPaneFocus on the new workspace's first pane after activation) - CONV-046 permits gesture-mounted focus; update TEST-664's sanction if the mechanism is requestPaneFocus rather than commitPane. propose CONV: A creation gesture that closes a picker/modal into a surface it just created MUST land keyboard focus in the created surface (or a named target), never on <body> via a focus-restore whose captured opener unmounted together with the invoking chrome.
resolution: Fixed: newOrkyWorkspace success tail requestPaneFocus(terminal pane) — focus lands in the cockpit terminal (the only registered focuser + the ready-to-type half), never body; all three gesture entry points consistent; menu-rename path untouched. TEST-679 green, TEST-683 witnessed green.

claim: The FROZEN e2e acceptance suite is RED against the built implementation (witnessed 2026-07-03: 4 passed, 2 failed) — CONV-052 forbids closing the review gate without one green run. TEST-677 (the flagship REQ-006 witness that the shared reportAssignment repair fixes ALL templates, on pre-F11 machinery) fails BEFORE its reload assertion: after clicking the saved Solo row, TemplatesMenu onPicked -> startRename renders the new workspace tab as a ws-rename input WITHOUT data-tab-id (WorkspaceTabs.tsx:61-69), so locator([data-tab-id]) stays at 1 for the full 20s timeout (expected 2). TEST-678 fails on a Playwright strict-mode violation: locator([data-testid^=tpl-], hasText CK) resolves to TWO elements, because a string hasText filter is CASE-INSENSITIVE SUBSTRING matching and CK occurs inside the always-rendered built-in row own label Orky project coCKpit… (its later data-tab-id count-3 assertion would then hit the same rename gap). Both are frozen-TEST defects, not implementation defects — TEST-673..676 pass live and the shipped workspace-templates.spec.ts was re-witnessed green — but the consequence stands: REQ-006 e2e durability half (a MENU-instantiated template surviving the did-finish-load re-push) has NEVER been witnessed green, and the review gate cannot close on a red frozen suite.
fix: Sanctioned test loopback amending the two frozen e2e tests, then re-witness the FULL suite green (CONV-052): TEST-677/678 count tabs rename-tolerantly (e.g. locator("[data-tab-id], [data-testid^=ws-rename-]") or press Enter after the menu pick to commit the inline rename), and TEST-678 selects the saved row unambiguously (exact-text regex hasText /^CK$/, or the tpl-<id> testid). propose CONV: An e2e locator that must resolve exactly ONE element MUST NOT rely on a string hasText filter (case-insensitive substring matching) that can occur inside a sibling row label — use an exact-text regex or a unique testid.
resolution: Fixed (tests loopback): the 2 frozen e2e defects corrected (TEST-677 counts tabs after the rename commits / by a rename-valid selector; TEST-678 locates the tpl row by exact testid not the hasText CK/cockpit substring + scopes the post-reload orky-pane to data-active). Formally WITNESSED: npx playwright orky-cockpit.spec.ts + loopback = 7/7 green against out/. CONV-052 satisfied.

location: 02-spec.md REQ-005 (acceptance, e2e clause); tests/e2e/orky-cockpit.spec.ts:10-13; 04-tests.md (Harness constraints honored)
claim: REQ-005 frozen acceptance mandates a live-PTY e2e vehicle — a terminal whose reported cwd equals the root (the status-bar/cwd chain) and whose transcript contains no auto-typed command after the prompt — that 0010 FINDING-017 had ALREADY determined unrunnable in this checkout (node-pty native binding not built; live terminal spawns fail environment-wide). The frozen tests substitute the saved-file argv/layout pin (TEST-673 header cites FINDING-017 per the work order; 04-tests.md records the substitution) WITHOUT a spec amendment, so the frozen spec now contradicts the frozen tests on the required vehicle — the same impossible-vehicle class FINDING-002 repaired for the registryDetail-spy half of this SAME REQ, missed for the terminal half (the CONV-018 class: an arbitration recorded only in the tests/charter leaves the frozen spec contradicting the frozen tests). Residual gap disclosed nowhere in the spec: NO runnable test in this environment witnesses the live terminal actually starting at the project root with nothing auto-typed; the property rests on the structural pins (no launch key, exact kind/shellId/cwd keys, no pty-write call site) composed with the shipped spawn chain (pty-manager.ts:19-28 uses cwd as a structured spawn option).
fix: In the doc-sync loopback, amend REQ-005 acceptance to name the FINDING-017-aware substitute vehicle (the saved-file cwd/keys pin + the structural no-launch/no-pty-write scans + the rendered orky-pane half) and mark the live-PTY vector as environment-blocked rather than satisfied — the CONV-018 propagation this REQ already received once for its other half.
resolution: Documented residual — same as FINDING-006 (the quality lens filing of the same REQ-005 spec-vehicle-vs-test mismatch). The saved-file argv/layout pins are the honest vehicle; the spec prose amendment is a CONV-018 doc note recorded in 04-tests.md.

claim: REQ-003 acceptance requires Escape/cancel from EACH of the four picker states (loading / failed / empty / member list) to leave the workspace count, every existing workspace, and quick.templates deep-equal — but the frozen tests exercise cancel from only the member-list state (TEST-656 cancels under a held 2-member snapshot; e2e TEST-673 Escapes the member-listing picker). No F11 test cancels the cockpit picker while the registry is loading, failed, or held-empty. The residual risk is bounded — cancel is state-independent by construction (every close path routes through the single resolveOrkyCockpitPick(null), store.ts:344-349, which creates nothing) and per-state cancel-commits-nothing is the shared picker F9 contract F11 inherits — but the acceptance clause as frozen is not what the frozen tests exercise.
fix: In the test loopback FINDING-008 already requires, extend TEST-656 cheaply: repeat the cancel leg under registrySnapshot=null/registryError=null (loading), registrySnapshot=null/registryError set (failed), and a held [] snapshot, asserting the same deep-equal invariants — or amend the acceptance clause to state that per-state cancel is inherited from the shared picker F9 pins and F11 pins only the request-resolution path.
resolution: Fixed: cancel-from-loading/error/empty picker-state coverage added (TEST-680/681/682, cancel is state-independent by construction — green regression pins).
